reviews.review_title: Love! Excited for full debugged version
reviews.review_description:
Really enjoy the mechanics of this game. Throwing the cards into the abyss and watching them explode and manifest is good fun. I enjoy that you collect and wear rings that have power. The mixed reality is super dope. 

It’s a bit buggy as expected to early access. Here’s a list of bugs (or poor UI that wasn’t intuitive)

1. I was only able to select the first character even though it appeared that two others were available. I can grab them and see them approach the platform but I can’t see their profile stats and I cannot select to play them. 

2. After playing as the first character, it seemed that my upgraded stats and cards for that character were not saved. 

3. Occasionally the MR would switch back to VR without prompt and I’d have to manually switch it back to MR. 

4. I wasn’t able to make a purchase with the merchant. Not sure if I just couldn’t figure it out or if it was being buggy. 

5. I know you can see an enemies intended next move which is cool, however I don’t always understand what the symbols mean and there doesn’t seem to be a way to see the effects of their next action like you can see the buffs and debuffs for your character on your right wrist. 

I’m excited to play it some more and I’ll update my review with any other potential bugs I find.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the detailed feedback, we really appreciate the time you put into this. Glad to hear you’re enjoying the core mechanics and mixed reality.
You’re absolutely right about the issues you mentioned. Most of them are already identified on our side. Just to clarify, the other characters are not playable yet, this is a demo in development with only one available hero, Heracles. We’ll make this clearer in the UI.
Character selection clarity, MR switching, merchant interaction, and enemy intent readability are all being actively improved, and progression and save systems are something we’ve just started rolling out.
We’ve also just pushed a new Alpha build with multiple bug fixes and UX improvements, and more updates are coming soon.
Thanks again for the thorough analysis. If you’re up for it, join our Discord so we can talk there and you can be part of the community as we keep improving the game.

reviews.review_description:
My screen spins around sometimes . My dragon just floats all over the place. 

Throwing items sometimes just doesn’t go to the enemy.

But this is Slay the Spire in VR and is addicting to play 7 hope they release the full game soon and fix the bugs

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We know some bugs like camera spinning, floating dragons, and missed throws are still happening, and we’re working to fix them. We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the Slay the Spire in VR feeling, the full game will be even smoother and more polished soon.

reviews.review_description:
Honestly, this game is insane. It mixes cards and virtual reality in a way that hooks you from the very first minute. Every time you play a card, boom! it turns into a real weapon you’ve gotta throw at your enemy. It feels super intense and never repetitive.

Fighting creatures from Greek mythology is a total spectacle, and when the gods drop rings with special powers, man, that’s when things get wild. It makes you think strategically, but also lets you enjoy the action like you’re right there in the middle of the fight.

In short: addictive, original, and with a vibe I haven’t seen in any other game. Totally recommended. 💯🎮

developer_response.body:
Wow, thanks a lot! Really happy you’re enjoying the mix of cards and VR. We put a lot of love into making it feel intense and different.

Our goal has always been to combine the strategy of deckbuilders with the action that VR & MR make possible, and it means a lot to know it’s coming through. Your words give us a big boost to keep pushing RogueGods forward!

reviews.review_title: Cool VR deckbuilder, cards come to life
reviews.review_description: It is awesome, please give us the full version. I never expected a card game in VR to feel this epic. The cards turn into weapons and the mix of card strategy with VR action is truly unique, it really feels like the strategic possibilities are endless. I can’t wait to try the other heroes. I’m already hooked, and I haven’t even played that much yet. The tutorial could be a bit clearer, but if they keep polishing and expanding this, it could become a major VR title. Highly recommended if you like deckbuilders like Slay the Spire but want something more physical

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the awesome review!  We're stoked you’re loving how the cards pop in VR and how the strategy and action come together. That “endless possibilities” vibe is exactly what we were going for. We’re also working on making the tutorial clearer so the gameplay is easier to grasp. Your support means a ton and keeps us pumped to make RogueGods even better!
